response.justification: The role demands 5–10 years of hands-on C++ development on Windows, which this candidate does not have — C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) plus an academic thesis, over a decade ago. The candidate's career is overwhelmingly web/backend work in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. There is no SQL Server or Oracle experience (only generic SQL/MySQL), and no fixed income, derivatives, or investment-banking domain work; RIDE Capital is the closest brush with financial services but is fintech, not capital markets. Strong communication and engineering fundamentals are evident, but the core mandatory requirements are unmet.
strengths: Strong communicator — numerous talks, workshops, and courses delivered
Solid engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development
Some financial-services exposure via RIDE Capital and Optilyz
concerns: Negligible C++ experience (~6 months, a decade ago) versus 5–10 years required
No SQL Server or Oracle; no Windows-platform development focus
No fixed income, derivatives, or capital-markets/investment-banking domain knowledge
